content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS. Let the set of strings STSTST be the set of all distinct contiguous palindromic substrings of SSS. Each element of STSTST has a `value` associated with it which is initially 0. You need to process QQQ queries which are of the following two types:


111 LLL  RRR  PPP  XXX: Add XXX to the values of all palindromes that are substrings of S[1...P]S[1...P]S[1...P] and are of the form T+S[L...R]+rev(T)T + S[L...R] + rev(T)T+S[L...R]+rev(T) where rev(T)rev(T)rev(T) denotes the reverse of TTT. Here TTT can be any string (possibly empty). It is guaranteed that S[L...R]S[L...R]S[L...R] is a palindrome.
222 LLL RRR: Print the value of S[L..R]S[L..R]S[L..R]. It is guaranteed that S[L...R]S[L...R]S[L...R] is a palindrome.

Input Format

The first line of input contains the string SSS.
The second line contains an integer QQQ - number of queries.
Next QQQ lines contain description of queries. Each line starts from integer typetypetype (1≤type≤21 \le type \le 21≤type≤2) - the type of the query.

If type=1type = 1type=1, then rest of the line contains integers L,R,P,XL, R, P, XL,R,P,X.
If type=2type = 2type=2, then rest of the line contains integers L,RL, RL,R.




Output Format
For each query of the second type, output the answer to it.
constraints: All characters are lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the string S (1 ≤ |S| ≤ 100, lowercase letters). The second line contains an integer Q (1 ≤ Q ≤ 100) – the number of queries. Each of the next Q lines describes a query. The first integer of a line is the type (1 or 2). If type = 1, the line contains four more integers L R P X (1‑based indices, 1 ≤ L ≤ R ≤ |S|, 1 ≤ P ≤ |S|, 1 ≤ X ≤ 10^9). It is guaranteed that S[L..R] is a palindrome. If type = 2, the line contains two more integers L R (1 ≤ L ≤ R ≤ |S|) and S[L..R] is guaranteed to be a palindrome.
content.outputFormat: For each query of type 2 output a single line containing the current value associated with the palindrome S[L..R].

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Initially, the set ST={a,b,c,bb,cc,ccc,abba}ST = \{a, b, c, bb, cc, ccc, abba\}ST={a,b,c,bb,cc,ccc,abba}. Initially, the value associated with all of them is 000.

Query 111: S[1...4]=abbaS[1...4] = abbaS[1...4]=abba. There does not exist any substring in S[1...3]S[1...3]S[1...3] of the form T+abba+rev(T)T + abba + rev(T)T+abba+rev(T). So no value is changed.
Query 222: Value of S[1...4]=abbaS[1...4] = abbaS[1...4]=abba is 000.
Query 333: S[1...4]=abbaS[1...4] = abbaS[1...4]=abba. The substrings in S[1...4]S[1...4]S[1...4] of the form T+abba+rev(T)T + abba + rev(T)T+abba+rev(T) are: abbaabbaabba. So value of abbaabbaabba increases from 000 to 228228228.
Query 444: Value of S[1...4]=abbaS[1...4] = abbaS[1...4]=abba is 228228228.
Query 555: S[2...3]=bbS[2...3] = bbS[2...3]=bb. The substrings in S[1...3]S[1...3]S[1...3] of the form T+bb+rev(T)T + bb + rev(T)T+bb+rev(T) are: bbbbbb. So value of bbbbbb increases from 000 to 322322322.
Query 666: Value of S[2...3]=bbS[2...3] = bbS[2...3]=bb is 322322322.
Query 777: Value of S[1...4]=abbaS[1...4] = abbaS[1...4]=abba is 228228228.
Query 888: S[5...5]=cS[5...5] = cS[5...5]=c. The substrings in S[1...6]S[1...6]S[1...6] of the form T+c+rev(T)T + c + rev(T)T+c+rev(T) are: ccc. So value of ccc increases from 000 to 133713371337.
Query 999: Value of S[5...6]=ccS[5...6] = ccS[5...6]=cc is 000.

solution.approach:
1. Generate all distinct palindromic substrings of S. Store each palindrome as a key in a hash map together with its current value (initially 0). 2. For a type‑1 query (L,R,P,X) let core = S[L‑1..R‑1]. For every palindrome p in the map we check two conditions:   a) p occurs completely inside the prefix S[0..P‑1] (simple substring search in the prefix).   b) p can be written as T + core + reverse(T) for some (possibly empty) string T. This is true iff |p| ≥ |core|, (|p|‑|core|) is even, the middle part of p of length |core| equals core, and the left part of p is the reverse of the right part.   If both conditions hold we add X to the stored value of p. 3. For a type‑2 query we simply output the value stored for the palindrome S[L‑1..R‑1]. The constraints are tiny (|S| ≤ 100, Q ≤ 100), therefore the O(Q·P) brute‑force checks (P = number of distinct palindromes ≤ |S|^2) easily fit into time limits.
Time Complexity: O(Q · N^2) in the worst case (N = |S| ≤ 100). Space Complexity: O(N^2) for storing all distinct palindromes.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) for the set of distinct palindromic substrings.
